ops.body:
Handoff for the daemonkit extraction program as of 2026-07-19. Shipped and green: daemonkit v0.1.0 (tag a5ed2e3); fusekit v1.5.0 (the hard-cut; fusekit-holder cask published live); cc-interact 4aad58c+a0578e7; cc-orchestrate f1f15ca; cc-pool 4e1050a+b13657c+d9edb3d (migration + widget release-collapse + CI greening); cc-notes needed no flip; authkit v0.2.0 (CallerCheck pin fix); Track A live-verified end to end (one-tap Touch ID round-trip, ccn fusekit task 3a5812f done). The master plan file: ~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-08/plans/we-want-to-extracct-cozy-ocean.md.

## STATUS 2026-07-19b — parallel-with-rc3 streams COMPLETE (areas 2-6)
A second session ran everything that could proceed without colliding with the rc3 chain. All green, all path-scoped, no tags/releases created, captain-hook + homebrew-tap untouched:
- **Area 2 (docs pass): DONE.** daemonkit conventions -> ccn doc 76e9c2c (daemonkit store, not tree markdown — placement directive); README refresh shipped (daemonkit b5c1a5f); per-track migration docs cc-interact 20fc08c / cc-pool 3762436 / cc-orchestrate 49da6a6; cc-interact CHANGELOG 742b47c + claude-pool CHANGELOG (path-scoped) shipped CI-green; cc-pool doc 7b7a53f re-audited (5/29 claims drifted) -> rewritten + doc_verify'd. capt-hook desktop-helper.qmd + session-reviewer cross-link authored + handed off as captain-hook-store doc 0feeb72 (track-k applies post-ship; track-k has now shipped the helper). a9c2d88 criterion 2e9f002 met.
- **Area 3 (Track S / synckit): DONE to local green.** Gate = CLEAN-PROCEED (gate report = fusekit doc 0cf0502). Shipped: Phase 1 tests+goldens synckit b35037f; Phase 2 rewire synckit b0b5351 (U2-U5+U7-B) onto wire.Framing/PeerFromConn/trust.Policy/proc.SingleEntrant/proc.Flock/daemon.StateFile/drain.Simple/proc.Backoff under the FROZEN wire (goldens byte-identical, sol byte-freeze audit clean, CI green). Sentinels aliased (hostregistry.ErrLockBusy=proc.ErrLockBusy). 979cca8 criterion 518d812 met. DEFERRED: U6 (version/skew/takeover eviction — separable phase 2, net-new capability + missing Installed-version reader), U7-A (serve plist via service.Agent — blocked on a daemonkit Agent.Render() seam, recorded in af0d6e7), and criteria 8c434ba (live converge) + 9f21ed1 (host cutover) — no cutover this session.
- **Area 4 (Track FD design): DONE.** Design doc landed = fusekit doc d725547, adversarially validated v1(REJECT)->v2(REJECT)->v3(ACCEPT). Resolves Q1/Q2/Q3: dead-owner adoption for both lanes (no live Transition, no proc.Spawn in the retire path — fork-storm safe; open-W untouched), spec-journal-as-boot-authority, in-memory-mirror-as-strike-authority (retire lane calls no Gate; wire byte-identical). 32df2a6 criterion 4d2ff5d met; 187740e + a1758f1 (p6 VM) stay PENDING as the eventual proof. Named daemonkit P1 additions the implementation needs (exit-armed external Transition + Mode field, Intake.Reopen, StrikeStore.PutTimes/Snapshot/ImportTimes, lease.Fence.Release narrowing + composite drain.Fence adapter).
- **Area 5 (Wave 4 security): DONE (this-session scope).** E1 spike -> GO on db24393: the purego verifier already ships CGO-free, LOCAL_PEERTOKEN is query-time-LIVE (XNU + live fork/exec demo), so the real fix is the XPC audit-token transport (a full future project, GO'd on db24393). E3 Swift PeerTrust hardened-runtime/injection-entitlement parity SHIPPED (daemonkit 756e04b->f28022c; sol review found+fixed a CFNumber(0) bypass + a non-dict fail-open; live E2E env-gated behind DAEMONKIT_TRUST_E2E). E4/E5 documented-limitation tests (TF1 live, TF2/TF5 skipped) + cross-team reject fixture SHIPPED (daemonkit 47565e2). ae67609's 3 items dispositioned (item 2 resolved; items 1+3 -> XPC transport / real 2nd-UID env); 9b9d2b8 met with honest gaps named.
- **Area 6 (daemonkit v0.2): housekeeping DONE.** ea0f944 (appexes token) fixed+closed (daemonkit ebc5070); af0d6e7 roadmap updated with the review-time + Track S additions (service.Agent Render seam, wire.Framing LF-cap semantics). cc-guides script adoption (NEW placement directive): scripts/test.sh + gen-version-xcconfig.sh centralized as cc-skills fragments (cc-skills 6bfcace, daemonkit e576364, fusekit b2784c1) — POSIX rewrite required (the fork-bomb ulimit guard is -u on bash/macOS, -p on dash; portable form + gotcha note 945fa36). The v0.2 API-gap roadmap (af0d6e7) is otherwise unbuilt.

Remaining after 2026-07-19b: area 1 (rc3 chain + rc-tag cleanup), area 7 (release-ordering loose ends), the fusekit-holder observed cask upgrade, and the deferred Track S / Track FD implementation phases above.

## 1. In flight at handoff (finish first)
- **cc-pool widget release rehearsal**: rc1 exposed the missing xcconfig stub (fixed), rc2 exposed the callee's profile-resolution ordering bug (-showBuildSettings never downloads profiles; only a real build does). The fix is in flight in homebrew-tap release-app.yml (build first, then validate the BUILT products' embedded profiles); after it lands: ship the tap, retag v1, push cc-pool v0.31.0-rc3, expect release-app fully green with the cask correctly NOT published (prerelease). Then delete the rc tags/releases. The rc numbering is deliberately below the real version ceiling (v0.59.0) — harmless for prereleases.
- **Track K (captain-hook karp)**: SHIPPED 2026-07-19 (captain-hook 5d5de25f, CI green incl. the new helper-test job). Gate + review verdict: captain-hook ccn note a7f3d46; wire contract doc 66026ba; docs-needed note 7046fe1. The release-app.yml prerelease-diff-base bug found in review is fixed (homebrew-tap b6c1154, v1 advanced). The karp spec is the plan file this-should-hav-a-curious-karp.md (NOT wise-snuggling-karp — codename collision). NOTE 2026-07-19b: the desktop-helper docs for this are authored + waiting at captain-hook doc 0feeb72 — apply post-ship (re-verify against what shipped; fold the dirty CHANGELOG [Unreleased] into a dated section; the SKILL-stamp item is dropped, the real delta is in references/*.md).
- **fusekit-holder observed cask upgrade**: v1.5.0's cask published; one observed live upgrade on a host completes Track F's last gate line (coordinate — a holder over live mounts).

## 2. Docs pass (fable prose; writing-docs + slop-cop on all of it) — DONE 2026-07-19b (see STATUS above)
- captain-hook: docs/guide/desktop-helper.qmd (+ session-reviewer.qmd cross-link), CHANGELOG entries (helper, --pr-title, review snapshot), scanning-sessions SKILL.md stamp updates to carry --pr-title. From Wave B's docs-needed list. [authored + handed off = captain-hook doc 0feeb72]
- cc-pool: ccn doc 7b7a53f already rewritten (newer-wins takeover + settle-before-cancel drain) — verify it against the shipped code once more at review. [re-audited + rewritten + verified]
- cc-interact/cc-orchestrate/cc-pool CHANGELOGs: entries for the migration behavior changes (EUID floor, 64MiB frame cap, newer-wins eviction, settle-before-cancel, KeepAlive SuccessfulExit=false). [cc-interact + cc-pool shipped; cc-orchestrate = no net-new needed]
- daemonkit docs/conventions.md + per-track docs + README refresh (the plan's cross-cutting docs line; task a9c2d88 in this store). [conventions -> ccn doc 76e9c2c; per-track docs landed; README shipped]

## 3. Wave 3 — Track S (synckit + cookiesync + reposync + host cutover) — impl DONE to local green 2026-07-19b (cutover deferred)
ccn task 979cca8 in this store; the plan's Track S bullet holds the full preserved-semantics list (frozen rpc Request/Response bytes, exclusive dispatch, 16MiB cap, same-UID-before-first-byte, exit-255-for-ssh, record-before-notify anti-echo, per-peer breakers, tombstones, monotone writes) and the no-outage host-cutover runbook shape (bootout synckitd FIRST, per-app agents, TCC surfaces, squatter recovery). Apply the recon-first contract: THREE prior tracks proved the plan's migration mappings can be optimistic — verify synckit's actual shapes against daemonkit before rewiring, and expect wire.Server NOT to fit frozen-byte consumers (use Framing/PeerFromConn sub-primitives, per cc-interact's precedent and the v0.2 note af0d6e7 in the daemonkit store). [DONE: gate report 0cf0502, rewire shipped synckit b0b5351; host cutover 9f21ed1 + live converge 8c434ba + U6 remain]

## 4. Track FD — fusekit-holder daemon on daemonkit drain (design-first) — DESIGN DONE 2026-07-19b
ccn task 32df2a6 in this store. fusekit's holder keeps skew-triggered self-retire + LaunchAgent relaunch today; the drain-consumer rework is the AGENTS.md-referenced multi-tenant holder plan. Design must resolve: the frozen retire wire fields, successor-spawn vs launchd under the fork-storm constraint, mount-spec journal vs canonical seq journal. Carries the drain-consumer contract checklist (daemonkit doc 67f6e62), gated on a fable design doc before any implementation. The deferred hello/health-before-proto-gate patch sketch for mountd is on the task. [DONE: design doc d725547 (v3, ACCEPT); IMPLEMENTATION (P0-P7, the daemonkit P1 additions, p6 VM proof 187740e/a1758f1) remains]

## 5. Wave 4 — security hardening — DONE (this-session scope) 2026-07-19b; XPC transport is the GO'd future project
- Go XPC/Mach audit-trailer transport replacing LOCAL_PEERTOKEN on code-identity surfaces: ccn task db24393 (this store). fable implements (security-sensitive); sol reviews. [E1 spike GO'd it as a full future project — verifier half already ships CGO-free; the transport half breaks the frozen wire, needs the consumer-migration story]
- Swift XPCTrust, TF9 (library-validation strictness), TF10 (fork/exec, PID-reuse, SCM_RIGHTS, setuid trust tests), cross-team reject fixture: daemonkit note ae67609 + P2 task 9a16f2e's one pending criterion (this store) enumerate them. [E3 Swift parity SHIPPED; TF10 = documented-limitation tests (unix transport can't reject same-UID substitution per E1); cross-team fixture SHIPPED; XPCTrust deferred into the XPC transport project]
- Also from ae67609: Go reads frames before trust (revisit with XPC); Swift PeerTrust lacks Go's Hardened-Runtime/injection-entitlement parity; Swift foreign-UID floor needs a cross-uid E2E. [item 2 RESOLVED (E3); item 1 -> XPC transport; item 3 -> real 2nd-UID env — all dispositioned on ae67609]

## 6. daemonkit v0.2 (all in the daemonkit store)
Note af0d6e7 is the roadmap: supervise's four gaps (persisted budget/ResetStrikes, confirmed-dead-without-probe, push entry, per-subject grace), Takeover's PIDExit-on-every-path + grace early-out + client identity binding, drain.Simple's cancel-order for long-lived streams, wire.Server consumer-envelope serving, KeyedMutex TryLock. Add at review time: service.Agent restart policy (SuccessfulExit:false — cc-pool adapts the plist caller-side until then), proc.Spawn ctx-check-before-launch (a dead ctx still launches today), a shared frozen-order JSON writer (three consumers now hand-serialize against Foundation's hash-ordered JSONEncoder). Plus task ea0f944 (stale appexes token in templates/release.yml.tmpl). cc-orchestrate task a315cc4 (supervisor-test teardown flake) and the cli/service.go ensureDaemon-onto-Takeover convergence (recorded on cc-pool's Track P trail) ride along. [2026-07-19b: ea0f944 DONE (daemonkit ebc5070); af0d6e7 updated with service.Agent Render-seam + wire.Framing LF-cap; the shared shell scripts are now cc-guides-managed (cc-skills 6bfcace / daemonkit e576364 / fusekit b2784c1) + dash-vs-bash ulimit gotcha note 945fa36. The rest of af0d6e7 is unbuilt v0.2 API work.]

## 7. Release-ordering loose ends
- cc-orchestrate's next release needs a cc-interact release first, then a require bump (its go.mod pins v0.11.0).
- Session task #17 (cask publish through release-app.yml specifically) is proven at cc-pool's first REAL (non-rc) release; the shared publish actions are already twice-proven (authkit, fusekit-holder).
- cookiesync's next release exercises its cask's new depends_on authkit chain.

Working discipline that carried the program: fetch-and-check origin before dispatching any track (memory: verify-track-state-before-dispatch); recon-first with stop-and-options on structural mismatch; sol refuter over every implementation before ship; disk is the record; ships via ccx vcs ship with hunk-scoping for shared working copies.
